hypothesis: Enforcing the corrected aromatic-aware valency constraints (indexed by element, formal charge, number of aromatic bonds, and non-aromatic bond order) as hard masks during sampling in a GEOM-Drugs–trained 3D diffusion model will reduce the mean GFN2-xTB relaxation energy of valid generated molecules by at least 20% compared to unconstrained sampling with identical network weights and denoising steps.
